mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.5%, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a random baseline.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of the variance in 60-day forward returns, or SPX forward returns remain positive when both INDPRO and HOUST decline simultaneously for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of contracting consumer credit on equity valuations, producing strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25 percentage points in a single meeting
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS rises, or SPX forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during Fed tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth rate signals expanding aggregate demand that directly supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with quarterly GDP growth revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for three consecutive months, it confirms a robust labor market that sustains consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS exceeds 200000 for three consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances following three consecutive months of payroll growth above 200,000, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising consumer credit balances and accelerating housing permits reflects a credit-fueled demand boom that temporarily inflates equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both RSAFS and HOUST rise, or SPX forward returns turn negative despite simultaneous credit and housing growth exceeding historical medians.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, rising industrial production fails to translate into equity gains because the Federal Reserve prioritizes price stability, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive when INDPRO rises alongside CPIAUCSL above 3.0%, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in predicting equity returns during high inflation periods.
